name: Italian Ice - Quick 'n Easy Syrup
recipeIngredient: 1 cup syrup (e.g., root beer, cotton candy)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix together 4 cups of water, 1 cup of syrup, and 1 cup of sugar.
2. This recipe makes about 6 quarts of Italian Ice using any home ice cream maker.
3. To make this recipe, use any snow cone syrup that you can find in most stores during the spring/summer. The Root Beer and Cotton Candy syrups are our personal favorites.
4. If desired, mix sugar with 1 cup of warm or hot water before combining everything.
5. Cool the mixture off in the freezer before placing it in the ice cream maker (optional but recommended for faster results).
6. Follow your ice cream maker's instructions to freeze the mixture into Italian Ice.
7. For a money-saving tip, buy a 50-pound bag of granulated salt (solar salt) instead of buying small boxes of ice cream salt.
Twist:
Use 4 cups water, 2 cups sugar, and any desired extract flavor for a variation on this recipe. Adjust the amount of extract to suit your taste preferences. Our favorite is chocolate extract.
